page: Amazon Web Services r8i.96xlarge is a memory-optimized server built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors with an x86_64 architecture, delivering 384 vCPUs across 192 physical cores at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 3072.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the top tier for multi-core CPU execution and memory bandwidth. The processor includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for accelerated matrix multiplication. In testing, the server achieved top-tier results in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Passmark database operations, and Redis SET operations, processing over 508 million operations per second. While prompt processing for large language models is strong, text generation speeds are average to poor due to the absence of dedicated GPU accelerators.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8i.96xlarge offers high resource density for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The massive 480 MB L3 cache and high-speed DDR5 memory bandwidth minimize latency for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and complex data-processing pipelines. It is highly suited for large-scale Redis deployments, high-throughput database operations, and memory-bound scientific simulations.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i.96xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 384 vCPUs and 192 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it provides 3072.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core, with a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. The server does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, including Redis SET performance. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. This instance is optimized for memory-intensive workloads such as large-scale in-memory databases, high-throughput web serving, and enterprise applications requiring massive RAM capacity and high-speed memory access without local storage requirements.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.96xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 384 vCPUs, 3072 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for high-throughput database and in-memory workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.96xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 384 vCPUs, 3 TB of DDR5 RAM, and top-tier database performance.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized server featuring three terabytes of DDR5 RAM and three hundred eighty-four vCPUs for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 384 vCPUs and 192 physical cores via Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors
3072.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated workloads
Best suited for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise applications
